Assembly: The assembly fPolPol2.1 is based on 88x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: Genome assembly of Donax variegatus. This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly of Donax variegatus (xbDonVari1) is based on 72X PacBio data and 26X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 82 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 6.45 Mb (with the largest being 0.355 Mb). Additionally, 139 regions totaling 66.913 Mb (with the largest being 12.015 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 4 haplotypic regions, totaling 7.5Mb, (with the largest being 3.6Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Pyraloidea; Crambidae; Evergestis
Assembly: The assembly ilEveAene1.hap1.1 is based on 58x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based on BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements ( https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).The W chromosomes was identified based on its single-copy status within a diploid assembly. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome W are unknown.
